evals.expected_output: FAIL — the diff validates password too (not in spec), and the ->fails() call on $request is wrong (validate() throws before you can call fails()). Phase 1 flag: password validation not requested. Phase 2 flag: logic error with $request->fails().

assertions.text: Issues a FAIL verdict, not PASS
Flags password validation as out-of-scope gold-plating
Does not suggest improvements beyond the failures found

evals.expected_output: FAIL — a whole new PaymentLogger class was created when a single Log::error() call in-place was all that was asked for. Classic gold-plating. Phase 3 should note tests not executed (no tests exist) — not assumed pass.

assertions.text: Flags PaymentLogger class as gold-plating / out of scope
Does NOT assume tests pass just because no tests exist
States tests were not executed or not available

evals.expected_output: FAIL — the 60% discount test (which directly validates the bug fix) is skipped with @skip. Only 1 test ran, not 2. The dev's claim of 'all tests pass' is misleading — the only test that actually validates the fix was disabled.

assertions.text: Identifies the skipped test as a problem
Notes that the skipped test is specifically the one validating the bug fix
Does not accept the dev's self-reported 'all tests pass' as verification
